examples: Cobalt ores found in China have a high manganese content, which produces a more muted blue-gray color.
Poor manganese may cause dwarfism, anemia, bronchial asthma, Parkinson's disease, child amentia, tumor, et al.
Higher than normal levels of manganese, strontium, and vanadium probably indicate a less nutritious diet heavily dependent on edible plants.
Chemical analysis of martian meteorites has revealed that Mars boasts more manganese than Earth.
Gabon depended on timber and manganese until oil was discovered offshore in the early 1970s.
